title: 3 Reasons to Buy Joby Aviation Over Archer Aviation
text: Joby Aviation offers superior long-term reward potential with its transportation-as-a-service (TaaS) model compared to Archer's original equipment manufacturer (OEM) approach. Analyst projections show by 2034, Joby's revenue of $11.0 billion and free cash flow of $1.28 billion both significantly exceed Archer's forecasts of $4.89 billion and $988 million respectively. Joby is slightly ahead of Archer in the FAA certification race, contrary to assumptions that Archer's OEM model would enable faster regulatory approval. This undermines the argument that Archer carries less development risk. While Archer faces uncertainty with reported hesitation from United Airlines about fulfilling its $1 billion order commitment, Joby benefits from continued investment by Delta Air Lines, suggesting institutional confidence in its business model and integration potential. Joby's TaaS approach involves greater complexity and longer timelines to profitability, but analysts increasingly view these risks as manageable given recent progress and partnerships.
